Journey Through Kerala¶

Business Problem:¶

A traveler interested in visiting Kerala wants recommendations for the best tourist destinations based on: Budget constraints,limited travel time, travel preferences (nature, beaches, hill stations, adventure, honeymoon, family trip, etc.)The challenge is to help the traveler choose an optimized Kerala tour plan that minimizes cost and travel time while maximizing travel experience.

Problem Statement¶

Analyze travelers budget, travel duration, and destination preferences to identify the most suitable places to visit in Kerala.Based on the analysis, recommend the Best tourist destinations, Budget-friendly travel plans, Time-efficient travel itineraries, Suitable accommodation and transport options, Personalized tour packages for travelers.

In [1]:
# import packages
In [1]:
import re
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

Webscraping Code¶

In [2]:
tour_package=[]
Days=[]
Nights=[]
trip_duration=[]
discount_percentage=[]
Discount_price=[]
Actual_price=[]
Hotels_included_package=[]
Cities=[]
customizable=[]
activities=[]
for i in range(1,62):
    url='https://traveltriangle.com/tour-packages/Kerala'
    headers={
    "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120 Safari/537.36"}
    page=requests.get(url,headers=headers)
    soup=BeautifulSoup(page.text)
    details=soup.find_all('div',class_="col-12 p8 pt0")
    for i in details:
        a=i.text   
        result = " ".join(a.split())
        #print(result)
        b=re.findall(r"^(.*?)Please deselect",a)
        tour_package.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r'(\d+)\sDays',a)
        Days.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r'(\d+)\sNights',a)
        Nights.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r'\d+\sDays\s&\s\d+\sNights',a)
        trip_duration.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r'from:(\d+%)\sOffExact',a)
        discount_percentage.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r'¹\s(.+)/-â',a)
        Discount_price.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r'-â\x82¹\s(.+)/-Per',a)
        Actual_price.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r'package:\s(.+)Cities',a)
        Hotels_included_package.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall( r"Cities:\s*((?:[A-Za-z]+(?:\s[A-Za-z]+)?\s\(\d+D\))+)",a)
        Cities.append(b[0])
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r'Customizable',a)
        customizable.append("Yes" if b else "No")
    for i in details:
        a=i.text
        #print(a)
        b=re.findall(r"sharing(.*?)(?:Best sellersWaterfall|Budget)?(?:Hotel included in package:|Cities:)",a)
        text = " ".join(b)
        split_names = re.findall(r"[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s[a-z]+)?(?:\s[A-Z][a-z]+)*",text)
        #print(split_names)
        activities.append(" ".join(split_names))
df=pd.DataFrame({"Package_name":tour_package,"Days":Days,"Nights":Nights,"Trip_duration":trip_duration,"Discount_Percent":discount_percentage,
                 "Discount_price":Discount_price,"Actual_price":Actual_price,"Hotels":Hotels_included_package,"Cities":Cities,"Customizable":customizable,"Activities_included":activities})
df
Out[2]:
Package_name Days Nights Trip_duration Discount_Percent Discount_price Actual_price Hotels Cities Customizable Activities_included

1 Charismatic Kochi Tour Package 4 3 4 Days & 3 Nights 9% 15,000 16,484 3 Star Kochi (1D)Munnar (3D) Yes Waterfalls Adventure Nature Shopping Wildlife ...

In [63]:
df.to_csv('Project Dynamic Web Scrapping')

EDA¶

This dataset contains no missing values and inconsistent values.¶

In [9]:
df['Discount_price']=df['Discount_price'].str.replace(',','')

df["Discount_price"]=df["Discount_price"].astype('int')
In [11]:
df['Actual_price']=df['Actual_price'].str.replace(',','')
In [12]:
df['Actual_price']=df['Actual_price'].astype('int')
In [13]:
df['Discount_Percent']=df['Discount_Percent'].str.replace('%','')
In [14]:
df['Discount_Percent']=df['Discount_Percent'].astype('int')
In [15]:
df['Days']=df['Days'].astype('int')
In [16]:
df['Nights']=df['Nights'].astype('int')

50 3 2 3 Days & 2 Nights 9 7840 8615

7. Display package name at 20th row using at¶

In [19]:
df.at[20,'Package_name']
Out[19]:
'Alleppey-Munnar Kerala Weekend Tour '

8. Display 12000 row and 0 column name using iat¶

In [20]:
df.iat[1200,0]
Out[20]:
'Alleppey-Munnar Kerala Weekend Tour '

9. Display 388 row and 5 column value using iat¶

In [21]:
df.iat[388,5]
Out[21]:
np.int64(5000)

Data Visualization¶

Univariate¶

In [16]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
In [ ]:
In [23]:
df['Discount_Percent'].plot(kind='hist',bins=50,title="Discount_Percent");

There is a high frequency of the 9% discount option.

In [24]:
df['Actual_price'].plot(kind='hist',bins=100,title="Actual_price");

Most of the packages have an actual price range of 15,000 to 17,000.

In [213]:
df
Out[213]:
Package_name Days Nights Trip_duration Discount_Percent Discount_price Actual_price Hotels Cities Customizable Activities_included

... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ...

In [212]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12,4))
sns.countplot(data=df,x='Trip_duration',palette='cool')
Out[212]:
<Axes: xlabel='Trip_duration', ylabel='count'>
In [46]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,4))
sns.histplot(data=df,x='Discount_Percent',hue='Hotels')
Out[46]:
<Axes: xlabel='Discount_Percent', ylabel='Count'>
In [233]:
plt.figure(figsize=(76,30))
sns.countplot(data=df,x='Package_name',hue='Discount_Percent')
Out[233]:
<Axes: xlabel='Package_name', ylabel='count'>
In [55]:
df['Cities'].value_counts().nlargest().plot(kind='pie',autopct='%1.1f%%',title='Cities');

Most people choose to visit Munnar and Alleppey

In [27]:
df['Trip_duration'].value_counts().plot(kind='pie',autopct='%1.1f%%',title='Trip_Duration');

Most people choose a trip duration of 4 days and 3 nights, while fewer people choose 8 days and 7 nights.

Bivariate¶

In [28]:
plt.figure(figsize=(15,4))
sns.barplot(data=df,x='Actual_price',y='Discount_price',hue='Actual_price')
plt.title(label='Actual_price Vs Discount_price');

Packages with an actual price above 30,000 are offered at a discounted price of up to 27,000.

In [56]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,4))
sns.barplot(data=df,x='Trip_duration',y='Discount_price',hue='Trip_duration')
plt.title(label="Trip_duration Vs Discount_price");

The actual price depends on the number of days and nights; fewer days result in a lower actual price.

In [30]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,4))
sns.barplot(data=df,x='Hotels',y='Actual_price',hue='Hotels')
plt.title(label="Hotels Vs Actual_Price");

Hotel pricing depends on the package, especially the number of days and nights. Some packages include 3-star hotels, while others may include 3-star, 4-star, or 5-star hotels depending on the package type and overall cost.

In [31]:
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

Multivariate¶

In [48]:
data = df[['Days', 'Nights', 'Discount_Percent','Discount_price', 'Actual_price']]
corr = data.corr()
plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
sns.heatmap(corr,annot=True,cmap='coolwarm',linewidths=2)
plt.title("Tour Package Correlation Heatmap")
plt.show()
In [138]:
sns.scatterplot(data=df,x='Days',y='Nights') # This plot shows a linear relationship between days and nights, i.e., the correlation coefficient is 1.
Out[138]:
<Axes: xlabel='Days', ylabel='Nights'>
In [65]:
df['Nights'].corr(df['Days'])
Out[65]:
np.float64(1.0)
In [43]:
plt.figure(figsize=(20,4))
sns.pairplot(data=df,hue='Trip_duration')
Out[43]:
<seaborn.axisgrid.PairGrid at 0x1980ccb82d0>
<Figure size 2000x400 with 0 Axes>

The pair plot of numerical variables indicates that some relationships are nearly linear, with correlation coefficients close to 1, such as 0.97.

Filter and display the best packages based on parameters like price, trip duration, and cities.¶

In [73]:
df1=df[(df['Actual_price']<10000)&(df['Days']<7)&(df['Nights']<6)]

In [193]:
sns.scatterplot(data=df7,x='Actual_price',y='Discount_price',hue='Discount_Percent',size='Trip_duration');

A 9% discount is applicable only for packages with an actual price above 30,000 and a trip duration of 8 days and 7 nights.

plt.figure(figsize=(10,4))
plt.subplot(1,2,1)
sns.histplot(data=df,x='Actual_price',kde=True)
plt.subplot(1,2,2)
sns.histplot(data=df,x='Discount_price',kde=True)
Out[30]:
<Axes: xlabel='Discount_price', ylabel='Count'>
In [26]:
sns.histplot(data=df,x='Discount_price',kde=True)
plt.xlabel('Discount_price');
In [37]:
top_discount_packages = df.nlargest(50, 'Discount_Percent')[['Package_name', 'Discount_Percent']]
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.barplot(x='Discount_Percent', y='Package_name', data=top_discount_packages)
plt.title("Top 10 Packages with Highest Discounts")
plt.xlabel("Discount_Percent")
plt.ylabel("Package_name")
plt.show()
In [41]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))
sns.scatterplot(x='Actual_price', y='Discount_price', hue='Discount_Percent', size='Discount_Percent', data=df, palette="viridis")
plt.title("Old Price vs. New Price with Discount")
plt.xlabel("Actual Price")
plt.ylabel("Discount Price")
plt.legend(title="Discount_Percent", bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left')
plt.show()

Conclusion¶

The analysis helps identify the most suitable Kerala tourist destinations by considering travelers budget, trip duration, and travel preferences. By evaluating factors such as destination popularity, Total costs, travel time, and package options, the solution enables travelers to select cost-effective and time-efficient tour plans without compromising their travel experience.The recommended approach supports personalized travel planning by suggesting ideal destinations, optimized itineraries, and budget-friendly packages tailored to different traveler needs such as family, adventure, nature, or beach trips. This enhances customer satisfaction, improves decision-making, and provides a more organized and efficient travel experience in Kerala.

In [ ]:
